items.title: Portland Community College faculty and staff unions go on strike
items.summary: The public institution went remote on Wednesday as unions issued a work-stoppage to win stronger pay increases for employees.

items.title: Screen-Free Schools? Some Legislators Push for a New Normal
items.summary: First, it was no phones in schools. Now, amid the debate around edtech, schools are looking to go screen free.

items.title: Portland State University could close 3 departments, trim over a dozen others
items.summary: The public institution is grappling with a $35 million budget hole, but some faculty criticized the plan for cuts as “hasty and unimaginative.”

items.title: Cal State sues Trump administration over Title IX funding threats
items.summary: The administration is threatening San José State University's funding over a transgender student-athlete's previous participation in women's sports.

items.title: University of Wyoming must review programs and staffing for possible cuts
items.summary: The public institution came out of a cut-hungry legislative session with its funding intact, but it now has a state mandate to look for reductions.

items.title: Ohio State University president resigns over ‘inappropriate relationship’
items.summary: Ted Carter abruptly stepped down from the public flagship this weekend and said Monday he had allowed "inappropriate access to Ohio State leadership.”

items.title: Culture at Columbia Shielded Sexual Assault by Physician, Report Finds
items.summary: Two doctors affiliated with the university and NewYork-Presbyterian Hospital stepped down after an external report found that the administrators mishandled allegations of abuse against Robert Hadden.

items.title: Judge Is Skeptical of Penn’s Argument Against Trump Demand for List of Jews
items.summary: A federal judge appeared receptive to the Trump administration’s demands for information about the University of Pennsylvania’s Jewish faculty and staff as the government pursues an antisemitism investigation.

items.title: Holistic Support Boosts Community College Outcomes
items.summary: Holistic Support Boosts Community College Outcomes Joshua.Bay Thu, 03/12/2026 - 03:00 AM A long-term study finds that students in Chicago’s One Million Degrees program increase their likelihood of graduating, securing stable jobs and earning a livable wage. Byline(s) Joshua Bay

items.title: College Student Athletes Using NIL, Transfer Portal to Speak Up for Social Injustice
items.summary: College Student Athletes Using NIL, Transfer Portal to Speak Up for Social Injustice Sara Brady Thu, 03/12/2026 - 03:00 AM The ability to transfer to less repressive playing environments gives student athletes the ability to disrupt injustices in our broader society. Byline(s) Shaun Harper

Juan Vargas (D-California). The funding was included in the House fiscal... The post Funding roundup first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: Workforce development is not a department — it’s higher education’s shared mission
items.summary: According to a March 2026 economic news release from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, the number of people not in the labor force but seeking employment remains at 6 million. items.title: This Is What Democracy Looks Like
items.summary: This Is What Democracy Looks Like sara.custer@in… Thu, 03/12/2026 - 03:00 AM Under pressure to teach American values, colleges can model civic engagement by joining efforts to strengthen at-risk voter protections. Byline(s) Sara Custer

items.title: Want to Attract Students? Try Improving the Weather.
items.summary: Want to Attract Students? Try Improving the Weather. Johanna Alonso Thu, 03/12/2026 - 03:00 AM Using data from a college in the Northeast, researchers found that prospective students who toured on a rainy or hot day were less likely to apply. Byline(s) Johanna Alonso

items.title: Hotline: Cybersecurity and Privacy | March 2026
items.summary: "Hotline: Cybersecurity and Privacy" tackles the philosophical, moral, strategic, and organizational quandaries related to higher education cybersecurity, privacy, and data. This month, Mike answers your questions about cybersecurity strategy, CMMC/CUI-compliant research computing and storage infrastructure, and the unchecked expansion of cybersecurity job responsibilities.

items.title: Are Wearables the Next Big Thing in Higher Ed Tech?
items.summary: Sophie and Jenay talk with a team from the University of Notre Dame about their research on using smart glasses to support accessibility in teaching and learning.

items.title: Careers Grow Where Community Exists
items.summary: Many young professionals struggle to find their footing as they build their careers. This conversation explores how community, mentorship, and shared experiences within higher education technology create confidence, spark growth, and open unexpected opportunities for those seeking connection and meaningful professional pathways.

items.title: The Penn Instructional Design Working Group: Professional Learning and Growth for ID Professionals
items.summary: At the University of Pennsylvania, the Instructional Design Working Group is blending professional growth and peer connection to transform how instructional designers learn, lead, and support one another. Its success offers a practical model that other higher education institutions can adapt to strengthen their own instructional design communities.

items.title: The Role of Faculty in the University of the Future
items.summary: In the age of AI, the true future of higher education lies not in replacing faculty but in freeing them to do what only humans can—build meaningful relationships, cultivate wisdom, and guide students through the ethical and intellectual challenges machines cannot navigate.

items.title: Smart Spending: Inexpensive Strategies for IT Professional Development in Higher Education
items.summary: By adopting practical, cost-effective strategies, higher education IT leaders can support continuous learning and career development for staff, even during budget-constrained times.

linkedin_drafts.title: Massachusetts community college policy watch
linkedin_drafts.text:
One Massachusetts development worth watching this week is MA Budget: Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002) (https://budget.digital.mass.gov/govbudget/fy27/appropriations/education/higher-education/71004002/).

Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.

What stands out to me is that community college policy in Massachusetts is increasingly about more than access alone. The deeper issue is whether institutions have enough advising, transfer, and wraparound-support capacity to convert access into persistence and completion.

Why this matters: Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.

For colleges, that is where the real work is.

#Massachusetts #CommunityColleges #StudentSuccess #AcademicAdvising #HigherEdPolicy

linkedin_drafts.title: Federal higher-ed policy watch
linkedin_drafts.text:
A federal higher-ed development on my radar this week is Public comment period for Workforce Pell regulations opens (https://www.highereddive.com/news/public-comment-workforce-pell-regulations/814247/).

The U.S. Department of Education issued its proposed rule to govern the expansion of Pell Grants to programs as short as eight weeks.

The most important thing about developments like this is not just the policy language itself. It is the operational effect on colleges: advising conversations, aid guidance, workforce programming, reporting obligations, and institutional planning.

Why this matters: Could affect aid eligibility, workforce pathways, grants, reporting, or institutional compliance.

Community colleges are often the institutions that feel these shifts first.

#HigherEdPolicy #CommunityColleges #FinancialAid #StudentSuccess #AcademicAdvising

linkedin_drafts.title: AI in higher ed: practical implications
linkedin_drafts.text:
One AI-related higher-ed signal I’m tracking this week is MassDHE News Clips: Community Colleges (https://www.mass.edu/newsclips/ccsystem.asp).

MassDHE’s community college clips often surface state-level transfer, workforce, affordability, and funding developments.

The real issue for colleges is not whether AI is coming. It is whether institutions can move beyond hype and define useful, governed, student-centered applications. That is especially important in advising, teaching, and student-support settings.

Why this matters: MassDHE’s community college clips often surface state-level transfer, workforce, affordability, and funding developments.

The colleges that benefit most will likely be the ones that combine experimentation with clear guardrails, staff development, and a strong sense of where human judgment still matters most.

#AIinEducation #HigherEdLeadership #AcademicAdvising #StudentSuccess #EdTech
